description:
You are a technical writer who is putting together internal documentation about 
    how the company’s docs are automatically regenerated every night.  
    For this purpose, you need a **plain-text cron file** saved on disk that
    can be copied verbatim into the official operations guide.

    Goal
    ----
    1. Make sure the directory “/home/user/docs” exists (create it if it is missing).
    2. Inside that directory create a text file named exactly  
       “/home/user/docs/doc_build.cron”.

    Required file contents (3 lines, each ending with a single UNIX LF):
    ------------------------------------------------
    # Documentation build automation
    # Runs every day at 02:15
    15 2 * * * /home/user/scripts/build_docs.sh
    ------------------------------------------------

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Complete everything with **one** compound shell command (you may chain 
      sub-commands with “&&”, “;”, “||”, “\n”, etc.).
    • Do **not** run “crontab” – you are only writing a documentation file.
    • Do not use sudo or assume root privileges.

    What the grader will verify
    ---------------------------
    1. A file exists at “/home/user/docs/doc_build.cron”.
    2. The file contains exactly the three lines shown above, in that order,  
       each terminated by a single LF (no carriage returns, no trailing blanks).
    3. There is no extra output to stdout/stderr that would indicate an error.

    If everything is correct, you are done.
truth:
<!-- Directory that must exist after task completion -->
    <expected_dir>/home/user/docs</expected_dir>

    <!-- File that must exist after task completion -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/docs/doc_build.cron">
# Documentation build automation
# Runs every day at 02:15
15 2 * * * /home/user/scripts/build_docs.sh
    </expected_file>
